summary: Recent updates to the Firefox browser have made several enhancements within the WebRTC integration, which is the technology that supports real-time communication features like video calls. Key improvements include changes that allow media codecs, such as the H.264 video format, to be created and managed more efficiently, using smart pointer techniques to ensure better memory management. Additionally, adjustments have been made to improve the accuracy of how the browser assesses media capabilities based on video resolution and frame rate, which helps optimize video quality. Another significant change is the configuration of audio bitrate and bandwidth estimation, which improves the overall performance of audio transmission during WebRTC sessions. These refinements collectively enhance the reliability and quality of real-time audio and video communications within Firefox, ensuring a smoother user experience.
